# Copilot Instructions

## Project Guidelines
- User prefers concrete, compileable implementations with complete support types and minimal generic planning language; when protocol details are unknown, use clearly marked provisional abstractions rather than vague commentary.
- Avoid endless brute-force reverse-engineering loops; switch to decisive implementation or explicitly name the blocker when progress becomes zero-sum.
- Use only synthetic public fixtures and independently observed interoperability evidence.
- Preserve uncertainty: classify fields as independently verified, compatibility assumptions, unverified, or reserved. Self-round-trip tests do not prove hardware compatibility.
- Do not add manufacturer executables, firmware, resources, screenshots, captures, decompiler output, function maps, personal codeplugs, or operational channel plans.
- Prioritize the Desktop GUI over CLI work unless explicitly asked for CLI changes.
